varieties.name: 1. Inga edulis ‘Standard Commercial’
varieties.origin: Central/South America, widely distributed, commercially available in Australia since 1990s
varieties.traits: Fast-growing evergreen tree to 15-30m height, broad spreading canopy, dark green pinnate leaves, white-yellow pompom flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits 2-3 times yearly. Mature trees produce 100-300+ pods annually under optimal conditions
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Trees, Fruitopia Nursery (Brisbane), Heaven on Earth Fruit Trees (FNQ), Greenpatch Organic Seeds, The Australian Plant Shop

varieties.name: 2. Inga edulis ‘Long Pod Selection’
varieties.origin: Amazonian Peru selection, documented 2m+ pods, cultivated 100+ years
varieties.traits: Large spreading tree 20-30m. Produces extraordinarily long pods, often spirally twisted. Bronze-green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, 200-400 pods per mature tree. Extended harvest season
varieties.availability: Specialty subtropical fruit nurseries, occasionally at Daleys as ‘Giant Ice Cream Bean’

varieties.name: 3. Inga edulis ‘Female Type’ (Black Seed Selection)
varieties.origin: Traditional Amazonian farmer selection, cultivated for centuries. Distinguished by deep black ‘female’ seeds
varieties.traits: Vigorous tree 15-25m, broad canopy. Seeds are deep purplish-black (vs. lighter olive tones in ‘male’ types)
varieties.yield_notes: Significantly higher productivity than ‘male’ types - Amazonian farmers preferentially propagate this form. 250-500 pods per tree
varieties.availability: Rare Fruit Club WA members, specialty imports. Request ‘female’ or ‘black seed’ type when ordering

varieties.name: 4. Inga densiflora ‘Brisbane Short Pod’
varieties.origin: Central America (Costa Rica, Venezuela), naturalized in Brisbane region 1980s-1990s
varieties.traits: Large attractive tree with spreading branches. Produces short hairy yellow-colored pods (distinctive from I. edulis)
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, fruits 2-3 times per year. Compact pods make harvesting easier
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Trees (often labeled as I. edulis), local Brisbane nurseries, propagates readily from seed

varieties.name: 5. Inga feuillei ‘Pacay’
varieties.origin: Peru (particularly Andean valleys), traditional cultivar, 500+ years cultivation history
varieties.traits: Rounded-depressed spreading tree 20-40’, more cold-hardy than I. edulis. Covered with white lenticels on young branches
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high productivity. Excellent fruit quality makes up for slightly smaller yields
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical fruit importers, occasionally available through Australian subtropical fruit networks

varieties.name: 6. Inga spectabilis ‘Machete’ / ‘Guaba de Machete’
varieties.origin: Ecuador, Colombia. Named ‘machete’ for the wide, flat pod shape resembling a machete blade
varieties.traits: Fast-growing tree to 40’, distinctive wide leaves. Spectacular white brush-like flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, exceptional quality. Pods are large and wide, providing substantial pulp per pod
varieties.availability: Rare, specialty importers only. AKME Gardens (USA) carries seedlings - international shipping may be possible

varieties.name: 7. Inga vera (Syn. Inga edulis var. vera)
varieties.origin: Tropical America, closely related to I. edulis. Some taxonomists consider it synonymous
varieties.traits: Medium to large tree 30-60’, spreading canopy. Very similar growth habit to I. edulis
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, 2-3 fruiting periods annually
varieties.availability: Available through specialist seed suppliers, sometimes marketed interchangeably with I. edulis

varieties.name: 8. Inga laurina ‘White Inga’ / ‘Ingá-mirim’
varieties.origin: Brazil (widespread throughout), Mexico to Argentina. Called ‘small ice-cream bean’ due to petite pods
varieties.traits: Smaller tree 15-20m, dense low rounded crown. Distinctive leaves set it apart from other Inga. Tolerates drier climates
varieties.yield_notes: Numerous small pods compensate for size. Very productive given tree dimensions
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers. Real Sweet Farms (USA) has seedlings - check Australian import options

varieties.name: 9. Inga cinnamomea ‘Guaçú’ / ‘Inga-açu’
varieties.origin: Amazon region (Brazil, Bolivia, Peru, Ecuador), riverine floodplain specialist
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree 10-25m, open crown. Thrives in wet conditions, waterlogging-tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in suitable conditions. Excellent sarcotesta (pulp) quantity
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia. AKME Gardens (USA) occasionally has stock. Consider seed imports from Brazilian suppliers

varieties.name: 10. Inga capitata
varieties.origin: Guianas, Eastern Amazonia
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree, similar growth habit to other Inga species
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity, valued locally but less commonly cultivated than I. edulis
varieties.availability: Very rare, specialist collectors only

varieties.name: 1. Inga edulis ‘Standard Tropical Form’
varieties.origin: Central/South America, naturalized across tropics. Available in Kerala since early 2000s
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree 20-30m, dense spreading canopy provides excellent shade. Rapid growth - 3-5m annually under optimal conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Outstanding in Kerala’s climate - fruits 2-3 times annually with 300-500 pods per mature tree
varieties.availability: Kerala Nursery (Ayush Kerala), Veliyath Gardens (Kerala), local agricultural nurseries in Thiruvalla region

varieties.name: 2. Inga edulis ‘Amazonian Long Pod’
varieties.origin: Western Amazon (Peru, Ecuador, Brazil), traditional selection over 500+ years
varieties.traits: Large tree 25-30m, broad umbrella-shaped canopy. Spirally twisted pods characteristic
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - mature trees produce 400-600 pods annually. Long harvest season
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers, international tropical fruit networks. Seeds Del Mundo (Europe) ships to India

varieties.name: 3. Inga edulis ‘Female Type’ (Deep Black Seed)
varieties.origin: Amazon basin, traditional indigenous selection. Seeds characterized by deep purplish-black color
varieties.traits: Vigorous tree 20-28m, dense foliage. Distinguished from ‘male’ types by darker seeds and superior fruiting
varieties.yield_notes: Dramatically higher than ‘male’ seed types - 400-700 pods per tree in Kerala’s optimal conditions
varieties.availability: Source from reputable Kerala nurseries with specific request for ‘female’/‘black seed’ selections. Import seeds from Amazonian suppliers

varieties.name: 4. Inga feuillei ‘Pacay’
varieties.origin: Peru (Andean and coastal valleys), centuries of cultivation, pre-Columbian heritage
varieties.traits: Rounded spreading tree 15-30m, more compact than I. edulis. Wide ecological range including drier areas
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, 200-400 pods per tree. Excellent fruit quality compensates for smaller size
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers. Has been introduced to Polynesia, Italy, California - Kerala introduction feasible

varieties.name: 5. Inga spectabilis ‘Guaba de Machete’
varieties.origin: Ecuador, Colombia, widespread in Andean foothills. Named for machete-blade shape
varieties.traits: Fast-growing to 30-40’, attractive broad leaves. Showy white brush flowers highly ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high productivity. Large wide pods provide substantial pulp volume
varieties.availability: Limited availability. Source through international tropical fruit networks or seed exchanges

varieties.name: 6. Inga vera
varieties.origin: Throughout tropical America, sometimes considered synonym of I. edulis
varieties.traits: Large spreading tree 30-60’, very similar to I. edulis in appearance and growth
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, multiple fruiting periods annually
varieties.availability: Often marketed interchangeably with I. edulis. Available through seed suppliers

varieties.name: 7. Inga cinnamomea ‘Inga-açu’ / ‘Guaçú’
varieties.origin: Amazon basin (Brazil, Peru, Ecuador, Colombia), riverine floodplain specialist
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree 15-25m, open crown structure. Thrives in waterlogged conditions
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in wetland/waterlogged sites. Superior pulp quality and quantity
varieties.availability: Rare. Source from Brazilian seed suppliers or botanical garden networks

varieties.name: 8. Inga laurina ‘Ingá-mirim’ / White Inga
varieties.origin: Brazil (throughout), Mexico to Argentina. ‘Ingá-mirim’ = small ice cream bean in Portuguese
varieties.traits: Compact tree 15-20m with dense rounded crown. More drought-tolerant than most Inga species
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific - numerous small pods. High productivity relative to tree size
varieties.availability: Brazilian nurseries, international seed exchanges. Veliyath Gardens may source on request

varieties.name: 9. Inga capitata
varieties.origin: Guianas and Eastern Amazonia
varieties.traits: Medium tree with typical Inga growth characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity, less commonly cultivated
varieties.availability: Very rare, specialist botanical collections only

varieties.name: 10. Inga edulis var. parvifolia ‘Cipo Brazilian Minor’
varieties.origin: Brazil, regional selection with dwarfing characteristics
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf variety with dwarfing behavior, smaller more manageable tree 8-15m. Maintains I. edulis fruit quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity for smaller tree. Earlier fruiting than standard types
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers. AKME Gardens (USA) carries this variety - explore Indian import options

varieties.name: 11. Inga edulis forma typica ‘Cipo Porto Yard Long’
varieties.origin: Brazil (Porto region), selection for extreme pod length
varieties.traits: Standard I. edulis growth habit, large spreading tree. Selected specifically for extraordinarily long pods
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, spectacular pod size increases pulp volume per pod
varieties.availability: AKME Gardens carries this variety. Brazilian seed suppliers
